title: Wrap text in column headings and/or cells
excerpt: Is there an option to have column heading and/or cell contents wrap in their cells?

posts.author: JS_CIC (Jo Spencer)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an option to have column heading and/or cell contents wrap in their cells?

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately not.

This has been requested previously:

Wrap Text in Column Platform discussions
Is it possible to set a column (like the item name) to wrap the text? We have many columns and would like to shrink the first column to see more across the screen.

posts.author: Marisa (Marisa)
posts.cookedHtml: wrap text on the top row would be useful, especially when the column criteria doesnt need to be wide - a better visual of your board

posts.author: jiminy (Jim Hourican)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would be extremely useful to make boards much easier to read and scroll through horizontally. I have boards with many columns. Many have longish column heading names, but the cell contents are only a few characters long. Thus the inability to wrap text wastes valuable real estate. This seems like a basic functionality in something like Excel. I hope we can get some more votes on this request and get it some attention.

posts.author: caseyparnis (Casey Parnis)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be so so useful to us. I have a board where many of the columns represent tasks that are very slightly different and key differentiators are difficult to see in the column header because the text doesn’t wrap, but making the columns wide enough to see the entire header is very unwieldy. I’ve tried everything I can to truncate the text but text wrapping is still needed.

posts.author: CUBRICON (Anthony Dias)
posts.cookedHtml: I just want to add another voice to requesting word wrapping. Please

posts.author: CJDC (Chris Coles)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature. I have several columns on a large sheet where the titles are long but the contents are small.

posts.author: gadbaw (Ben)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 This would unblock us. It’s a must have to choose Monday over Coda.

posts.author: CJDC (Chris Coles)
posts.cookedHtml: Any feedback from monday.com on when this might be a feature ?

posts.author: SRandall527 (Steven Randall)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another voice - this is a much needed feature - please help!

posts.author: DanaPloof (Dana Ploof)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this basic feature

posts.author: robgibbs (Rob Gibbs)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 Here…Please prioritize this.

posts.author: KevG (Kev Galway)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 here too please Monday!

posts.author: rkaplan (Richard Kaplan)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 Here too please Monday.com

posts.author: Natalie (Natalie Brady)
posts.cookedHtml:
Column readability is paramount in a tool such as Monday.com
I can’t emphasise enough how important these seemingly small improvements will be to most users!

posts.author: KarenK (Karen Kerr)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 here. Without this, it makes the review of information submitted by the Forms view almost unusable.

Also or possibly instead, could a widget be created that allows you to pull out cell contents into something presentable as a report (e.g. to visualise information that’s put in through a form - e.g. into a Project Initiation document type format or a Status Report format that I can pdf)…

posts.author: renaudsprkl (Renaud)
posts.cookedHtml: +1, we use monday as a product backlog with our clients and we like to write user stories. This would make the whole board much more readable if we could wrap the text automatically according to the size of the column instead of trimming the overflowing text.

posts.author: bbatz (Brian Batz)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for me on this being an important item.

posts.author: hfaucette (Heidi Faucette)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 here as well. SO NEEDED!!

posts.author: JMWIlliams (Jennifer Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know if this functionality is being developed. It makes me crazy changing the column width constantly

posts.author: hjcashin (Hayley Cashin)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please - this would be a huge plus!

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml:
A wrap text option and/or some way to easily view (and format / output) whole fields is a sorely needed basic functionality missing in Monday. This request has 66 votes, 20 replies, and was posted two years ago. Yet there is no response from a Monday team member.

This feature request was prompted by an earlier FAQ thread on the same topic that had many additional votes. @TRB-monday.com replied in that post August 2020 saying it’s not currently possible but should be submitted as a feature request. @TRB-monday.com can you please provide a status update on this requested functionality?

Some possible solutions that come to mind (in addition to basic text wrap) are a new type of form / card / report view where selected fields are dynamic and can be sized to show all the text they contain, including vertical scroll bar. Similar to forms and reports in MS Access. Ideally, this could be custom formatted per user preference for viewing and output to a printer / PDF etc. My team and many others really need this. Thank you.

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: Posting here to remind @monday that this is still something that would be nice to full time users

posts.author: carlyamlrs (Carly AMLRS)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com @monday-moderators @monday-team @CSG @CXS Any update?

posts.author: MGrabosk (Michael Grabosky)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be of great value and not something I would think would take monday developers very long to do. PLEASE implement word wrap in Board Headers!

posts.author: jyem (Janet Yem)
posts.cookedHtml: I am a new Monday user and that is the very first thing I noticed that I can’t do and it makes my board not as easy to read.

posts.author: mezzaong (Meryl)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 on this feature - I’m a new monday user so this would be really helpful for us especially for our notes

posts.author: mwithrow (Maddie)
posts.cookedHtml: Please, this is so necessary - wrap text on calendars, especially. I can only see the first few words of each task in calendar view, which is so limiting.

posts.author: pellegrino (Jeffrey C)

posts.author: jessBHA (Jess)

posts.author: KaterinaR (Katerina Rudkovskaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

Please join our discussion here: How do you work with tables in monday?
We’ll be very glad to know more about your use cases and needs.

posts.author: nancylambert (Nancy Lambert)
posts.cookedHtml: I think this is necessary as well.

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m new to Monday and baffled by the amount of apparently easy to solve shortcomings, like this one. Count me in!

posts.author: bgreen (Betsy Green)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature.

posts.author: mikerorkGC (Mike Rork)
posts.cookedHtml:
Story: We would like to add key information related to a project board at the top of the page which links to other tools our team uses. We’d like to format this information to highlight certain items and also allow for clickable links.

The description field under the board title seems like an appropriate section/field that has greater potential. We could use that area/field to link to other key information related to the project that exist in other systems.

  • We are forced to maintain another area in Confluence to hold some simple information that we want hold in Monday.com, with nice formatting and clickable links.

  • We tried experimenting with a new LINK column in the board, but it takes up too much precious space for in the entire board view for only a couple of links we want at the top of the project.

posts.author: sisrael1 (Sam Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, we have the same need, and the exact same use cases. We use Confluence, Jira, and other collaboration platforms. We also use Microsoft 365, but our company hasn’t approved the integrations yet.

Even when they are integrated, I still see a lot of value in being able to list the links to key assets and references for a board right at the top.

posts.author: mir4bell4 (Mirabella)
posts.cookedHtml: My company could use this as well. Rich formatting for board descriptions would allow us to link to other tools that we use besides Monday.com, especially ones for which an integration is not otherwise possible/easy to create. This actually ties in to my request for Rich Formatting for Form Field Descriptions - rich formatting all around would accomplish a lot of goals!

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d like to simply use this space to add a reminder to other users. It would be nice to be able to adjust simple attributes like font size/bold/character case, etc.

In our use case, we utilize our Boards to schedule deliveries. Each board is for a different day. We also use a general board to track Inquiries. I’d like place a bright, bold note in the board description reminding our scheduler to let users know that they can leave reviews for our services. Something big and bold that he can see when he is on the phone with them.

posts.author: chafic (Chafic)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with this entire thread. It would be very convenient to have HTML / Hyperlink support in the description box for each Monday board for those of us who use several linked boards. My use case would simply be an index of all linked boards in one spot.

posts.author: mikerorkGC (Mike Rork)
posts.cookedHtml: I wonder if, after 1 year, a representative from Monday.com will provide feedback on this request

posts.author: nikkige (Nikki GE)
posts.cookedHtml: We would also strongly support the need for this feature - it would make sharing boards for projects a lot easier as we could provide context and links to further related sites (eg SharePoint) where necessary rather than trying to sew this together another way.

posts.author: giulliasacco (Giullia Sacco)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am also waiting the same feedback/update on this feature. Same need over here.
Advanced Board Description with hyperlinks.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: And I came to the Community to make this exact suggestion. Please to see I’m not the only one. Our Board descriptions are getting quite long and unweidly!

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this where the ‘docs’ feature is going? This is somethign Clickup does very well

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml: Also, I just put a note in a board about one of our trucks requiring service and thought it would be beneficial to be able to quickly “Search Everything” to look for when the truck was requiring service, but found that the Description Field is not included in Search Everything results…this would also make that area much more useful.

posts.author: KGae (Konstantin Gaertner)
posts.cookedHtml:
push

Also VERY interested in this feature. Should not be too complicated I think, but very useful.
Any updates by Monday.com?

posts.author: chbroussard (Chris Broussard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump. Adding my voice in favor of this. To give team members context for a campaign or project, I’d really like to be able to put the goal/objective at the top.

Additionally, I want the Form page to be easy accessed, and since the Form View takes users to the editable form instead of the usable form for some reason, I’d like to be able to add a Form link at the top, too.

posts.author: cat.goliath (Cat)
posts.cookedHtml: I am in need of linking users to an NDA form that they must sign and upload. I can accept their files, but currently I have to type “please copy/paste this URL into your browser to download” which is clunky. All I need is rich formatting to make it a hyperlink.

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml: As a user, I would appreciate the ability to format the Text and Long Text columns either in rich text or HTML. Please ad an option achieve this (perhaps something like TinyEditor ?)

posts.author: Naomi (Naomi Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this would be really helpful for us too! We often detail minutes and highlight the important bits in bold red to ensure a quick sweep of the eyes catches these words. If we could do this in Monday.com it would move another vital tool over to this service from our current sharepoint site

posts.author: ADMoss3 (Arlene Donley Moss )
posts.cookedHtml:
Very new user here.

Any update on this?

I am using the long text in an email and I would love to have it be a bulleted list. Am I missing some other obvious column type that would hold a bulleted list that could then be used in an email?

Any help is appreciated. Thanks!

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml: Also using long text to personalise my automated email templates. This would be an awesome feature!

posts.author: rdeelen (Rohan)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes having this would be amazing. Right now I would like to add a description to a text field, but it just looks messy.

posts.author: Fcampos (Fernando)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this. Having to dig deep into a task items for a richer text is really bumming.

posts.author: Majormind (Tobias Brücker)
posts.cookedHtml: We need especially the possibility to process text longer than 2000 letters/characters. At least an option to change the maximum .

posts.author: erikdakoda (Erik Dakoda)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is an absolute must and a huge oversight for a platform as broad and mature as Monday. There is just no easy way to add a rich text description for a pulse!

  • You can add an update, and pin it to the top, and the conversation icon shows the number of updates. BUT, only the person who created the update can edit it. Useless as a description.

  • You can create an Item Card, fantastic, and you can add a collapsed long text field that you could edit in the card, great! BUT no rich text. Useless as a description.

  • You can add an Info Box Note, name it “Description”, and you can add nice rich text. BUT, an unselected pulse has no indication that it has any Info boxes. When you select the pulse, Updates is displayed by default (or the Info Card if you made it the default). There is no way I can find to make Info Boxes the default tab. I have tried this method for adding descriptions before, but people kept missing the description info box. And it takes two clicks to get to what I consider to be the most important part of the pulse. And - btw - Info Boxes are DEPRECATED.

I feel that the lack of a quick, easy, rich text description for pulses is the biggest problem with Monday and it has me looking at alternatives.

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? Is this where ‘docs’ is going?

posts.author: gregg (Greg Galloway)
posts.cookedHtml:
A variation on this request would be to be able to select the text standard for the field.

For my purposes, I would like to be able to write HTML into the field (I’m using the column to populate other resources via the Monday API). It wouldn’t have to be rendered, just saved. Right now, the field strips out any HTML I add as soon as I leave the board.

It would be great to be able to select that a Long Text column be raw text (and not filtered) or Rich text.

posts.author: Norm (Justin Furano)
posts.cookedHtml: Just want to stress how helpful this would be.

posts.author: OrionPax (OrionPax)

Yes please!!
Why doesn’t this exist yet??

posts.author: Dran (Daniel MacLean)

posts.author: kevinschmitt (Kevin Schmitt)
posts.cookedHtml: Please implement this feature asap. We really need it!

posts.author: NikolaMastilovic (Nikola Mastilovic (SpicyTab))

Hey guys!

We recently released the app that does exactly this! Feel free to check it out here: https://monday.com/marketplace/10000030 and let us know if you have any feedback!

posts.author: jmadrone (Jasper Madrone)
posts.cookedHtml:
Pretty funny that you can

write HTML

in this editor in a safe way, but it is disallowed in the actual app. My use case has nothing to do with HTML, I want to just be able to use the <> brackets at all. Anything inside them gets deleted. I am trying to use a long text column to store a preformatted message I will send to a slack channel. I want to format a link for slack by using their notation of <www.url.com|display text>, but that gets deleted from the long text field when you try to save it.

Please let me use the brackets.

posts.author: Andreea (Andreea)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yessss, we SO need this! I cannot believe that such a smart platform as Monday does not have this feature yet! And the long text field itself is annoying - you can only make it longer, not wider, which is a pain in the butt when you want to read or write a longer paragraph.

Also, what’s with the character limit? Long text columns have a 2000-character limit, while the “regular” text column seems to have no limit

posts.author: Bill_In_Ohio (Bill Duncan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Again, to add to the community’s thoughts already, we need this feature. I am trying to move to using this “Work OS,” but can’t seem to add bullets to a text field. I communicate with other departments, and when sending out an agenda or minutes, which is great to track and store in monday, we can’t seem to use bullets. My documents then have to be “converted” and then re-uploaded as a new version.
It seems that there are many ways to do this, and no matter what database the information is stored in, there are ways to make this happen.
Please, let’s get this ability into the software.

posts.author: Logan (Logan Ruths)
posts.cookedHtml: It is astonishing that this has yet to be addressed! Let the people edit their text as they like!

posts.author: jh_delegate (Jay Herrera)

It’s an industry standard and all of your competitors have it:

  • Trello
  • Asana
  • …

Now doesn’t that look nice?

GET IT TOGETHER MONDAY!!!

posts.cookedHtml: Hey @dean_m - Can you email us your use case to support@spicytab.com? Thanks!

posts.author: johnners (Drew)

posts.author: Dana_Shemer (Dana S)
posts.cookedHtml:
Really important feature!
Currently, we have to use the ‘Updates’ section instead which prevents us from having any structure to our discussions/summaries

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: So it looks like you can now add a Monday Doc as a column. I think this basically accomplishes what we need? Anybody see a downside?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Great idea and thanks to everyone for sharing feedback and thoughts. We’ve discussed the request to be able to wrap text in column headings and cells with the development team. Although they agree with the need for this feature, as of now it is not immediately planned, due to other priorities and team capacity.

We would love to keep hearing how this feature would impact your workflows and will update with any development changes. Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback makes a difference.

Have another idea? Submit it by opening a new topic!

posts.author: dean_m (Dean Marshall)
posts.cookedHtml: @mchinsky @Julietteb The feature seems great but if you can’t access it through the API it shouldn’t exist. Personally I believe when you add any feature to Monday the API access portion of it should be available at the same time or within a short time afterwards. I apologize in advance if I am mistaken on API access to documents.

posts.author: horosam (Samuel Horowitz)
posts.cookedHtml: No - because using the Doc column in an automation usually just ends up getting translated into a link to the doc rather than its contents.

posts.author: horosam (Samuel Horowitz)
posts.cookedHtml: We frequently need to send an email to set of contacts stored in a Board, based on various filter criteria. Perhaps committee memberships stored in a tags field, or people who are missing a particular field, etc. These are always one-off messages, so truly automated solution does not work, and there could be dozens so the basic gmail/Outlook integration does not work. Support pointed me toward a workaround using a Button, which can be combined with a field where an email could be composed to represent the content … but there is no way to use rich formatting. Using a Monday Doc just emails a link to everybody. So, the real paint point for us is no way to select multiple records for a simple copy/paste OR access multiple email contacts at once, but this seems like a more generally applicable workaround.

posts.author: DStout (Derek Stout)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a very easy ask to implement. Wrapping text is already available in columns such as “Long Text”. Why hasn’t this been implemented yet, after YEARS of asking? I’m guessing it’s a simple click of a button to apply the option to all columns.

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Our company develops the Smart Spreadsheet for monday app - hope it will help you to use Excel-like features right on monday.com platform.

You’ll be able to import/export your data (and boards with items as well) and work with it as a fully functional spreadsheet (cell formulas, conditional formatting, pivot tables, etc.) in one place.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: debbieg (Debbie Gane)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a frustrating feature to still be waiting on! When I’m exporting status reports half the information gets cut off from our risks and issue registers. Whilst you can see more info in each cell if you move the cell size to triple cell, that then means you can see a fewer number of risks/issues before it gets cut off where it starts to scroll

posts.author: Lucas1 (Lucas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Another no brainer. Headings become unreadable very fast unless you make the column wider, using up a lot of screen space only to completely show the headers, making the overview worse. Ability to wrap and/or turn the headers vertically like in Excel would be great for user experience.

Also we would like to improve some boards that have a lot of text input: for example from the use of forms, but to display the text you have to click the cell or make the column very wide, wrapping it or actually use the double/triple line function the items have would be very welcome as well:

posts.author: Kchase (Kchase)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be extremely useful.

posts.author: mzona (Marisa Zona)
posts.cookedHtml: Header row word text wrap in column headings is critical to user experience. We are new customers and this is driving people crazy.

posts.author: Cotters (Martin Cottingham)
posts.cookedHtml: I am new to Monday.com, it is unbelievable that text can’t be wrapped in columns & that the column widths are so limited. I am using the risk register template, it is impossible to read the information against a risk on the single page. It is quite unusable.

posts.author: jiminy (Jim Hourican)
posts.cookedHtml: Header row word text wrap in column headings is critical to readability, usability, and basically the entire user experience. Please, please, please implement this!

posts.author: Mel_Schulien (Mel S.)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this feature as well!

posts.author: sarah.thebay.church (Sarah Poindexter)

posts.author: amiraenergyedc (Amira R)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this feature! +1 +1 !!! you can adjust the size of the items but it still doesnt do anything for the header txt. having to hover is frustrating and it can take quite a bit of precision to get your mouse to hover correctly to see the full text. its quite challenging for some of the other members of my team. help us!!

posts.author: Ginny17 (Ginny)
posts.cookedHtml: Cant agree more to wrapping text as a feature! I have created a board to use as an HR Directory, and hovering over text to view peoples addresses is tricky and time consuming. Please bring this feature in!

posts.author: 949161 (MARIA MONTEVERDE)
posts.cookedHtml: yes please this feature would be great

posts.author: Graeme (Graeme Renfrey)
posts.cookedHtml: I wouldn’t describe being able to wrap text in a column field as a ‘feature’. Its more like a fundamental requirement. posts.author: skmdc (SK)

posts.author: vietminhk14 (Viet Do)

posts.author: alexlofthus (Alex Lofthus)

posts.author: Rosh400 (jonathan@dinamicaamericas.com)

posts.author: PayWay369 (paytonwest@hotmail.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Suggestion: Monday should create a smaller team of developers (hopefully QA and/or automated script’s running) that rotate members each cycle (you don’t want them to get brunt out) where these handful of individuals work on the smaller features, bugs and minor enhancement requests.

By having a dedicated team with rotating members, this will ensure the backlog is kept clean and attention is given to the lower level items. This also helps with the p2 and lower bugs that become stuck in the black hole.

posts.author: mchurchill (Michael Churchill)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a great feature to allow for better screen management via column widths.

posts.author: Dinah (Dinah Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: Am upvoting this - this is a key frustration for us using monday.com and I’ve been lurking for year on this topic. Like many commentators have said, having wrap text in the column header would be very useful to help others using the board and understanding what it was conveying without having to constantly hover or extend a column - it’s these small barriers that often lead to other team members not wanting to adopt monday.com and saying that they can do it faster in excel. Could we have a feature that expanded and closed columns Hoping for some dialogue on this.

Would also be great to be able to add bullet points inside a cell that wrapped so we could have jotted notes immediately seen for each task - and ability to customize the text style inside that cell.

posts.author: vogelb83 (Brian Vogel)
posts.cookedHtml: We feel that the ability to wrap text in a cell and have the row adjust its height so that all text is visible would be a really valuable feature. Seeing preview text and needing to use a scroll to see the full contents of a cell is cumbersome and makes this difficult to use for content and social media planning.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @vietminhk14,

We certainly aren’t ignoring the requests! We took this request directly to our product team last quarter who confirmed that this particular feature is under consideration as shown in the existing feature request here → Wrap text in column headings

If you’re happy for us to merge your request into the existing request to put greater emphasis on the need, we can definitely do so

posts.author: sivarajeswaran (Sivarajah Rajeswaran)
posts.cookedHtml: I will like to see the full item title when I create an item. Its best to wrap the text rather than showing only part of the title

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community, I know that his has been an important request for many and after reading all your feedback and sharing with the product managers, this request has gone from “under consideration” to "on roadmap". The ability to wrap text will be available on all boards soon! In addition to the ability to wrap column text, our boards team is working on a variety of features including improvements to time tracking column, friendly Item ID (improved version of Auto number), ability to re-order views and more! Since this feature is being developed along many other boards enhancement, we do not have an exact timeline for this feature but will update as soon as we know more!

You can make sure you stay up to date by turning on notifications for this topic.

posts.author: rose (Roseangeli Ayson)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s 2024! So is it here yet?

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
Amber here from the product team. As Juliette mentioned, we have added this request to our roadmap and are hoping to release it in the second half of the year!
Looking forward to getting this feature out to you as soon as possible while making sure we maintain the best user experience

posts.cookedHtml: Hi Amber! That’s good news, thank you!

posts.author: MattFrost (Matt Frost)

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Everyone,

We understand the need for the feature especially to improve board readability and efficiently utilize space. Due to other priorities and features in development, we do not have an expected timeline for this feature.

The boards team is currently focusing on finishing the development of other highly requested board features, such as the ability to reorder views, wrap text, and undo capabilities. Once these features are completed and released, our team will be able to continue to look into the best way to build a solution to wrap text in column headings and cells.

posts.author: Cassie (Cassie Boyce)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree! This is something our whole team are asking about and some people are not wanting to move away from spreadsheets as this feature is not available.

posts.author: Brenu (Brenu Nsiah)

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team. We’re excited to let you know that wrap text in cells is planned for release by Q2 2025! This feature will be available for text column cells (short text and long text) in both the table view and the table widget.

We know how valuable the ability to wrap text in column cells is for improving board readability and making the most of your space, so we’ve been working hard to prioritize this feature.

Thanks so much for your continued feedback - it plays a huge role in shaping the future of monday.com. We’ll keep you posted as we make progress!

posts.author: KlipRSA (Petrus)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s good news, but why does the topic state ‘solved’ if not available yet?

posts.author: kwiatks (Stefan)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 to this feature. I have a Text field that has only about 20 words in it but it’s very hard to read it without scrolling. Am looking at a Dasboard widget to just show all the text but it should not be this hard to see all of the text

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml: please please make this available for all column headers as well! (that is the name of the thread here and what the community is voting on…)

posts.author: ShelleyDonovan (Shelley Donovan Ritchie)
posts.cookedHtml: Any Update on release date? Where can I get a good summary of where the feature will exist. Assuming applicable to all boards and not just some.

posts.author: TDGregory (Tabetha)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love an update on this feature. The lack of ability to wrap text has been a major issue for my teams.

posts.author: cptbrau (Hal)
posts.cookedHtml: When will this be released??

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! I wanted to share a quick update on the wrap text in cells feature.

This feature is now planned for release in Q3 2025, as our team took some additional time to ensure we’re building the best possible experience for this feature. We can ensure great progress has been made, and the feature remains on track for Q3 delivery.

We know how valuable this is for improving board readability, and we’ll be sure to keep you posted as we get closer to launch. Thanks again for your continued feedback and patience!

posts.author: DavidatWISE (David R Currie)
posts.cookedHtml: For my use case, I urgently need the ability to wrap connect board columns so I can easily see all connected Items without hovering over the field. Notion does this quite well and I am migrating from Notion

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @amybronstein always disappointing to have feature releases moved out, but appreciate the information. Helpful to know it is still on the roadmap and the updated target release.

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just saw this has been released — at least partially!

You can now enable text wrapping in text columns, which is definitely a helpful step forward. Thank you @Monday team!

However, it’s important to note that this doesn’t yet apply to other column types (like formula, address, etc.), and — most relevant to the original request — column headings still don’t wrap.

Since the title of this thread specifically mentions wrapping column headings, I’d suggest keeping the status open or marking it as “Partially Released” until that functionality is included.

@amybronstein another thing- when you want to edit the cell, the edit still has to happen within a small pop out window, but editing within the (now larger) original cell would be so much smoother.

Appreciate the progress so far and looking forward to the rest!

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml: Turned this on when I received the pop-up notification. I want to turn it off, but couldn’t find the on/off settings anywhere. You must go to the three-dot icon, and change the item height to “single.”

posts.cookedHtml:

Can you please make it so that item names have the wrap text option too, specifically for the mobile experience.

posts.author: Br0ck (Brock Jaeger)
posts.cookedHtml: Additionally, you can’t save it?! You can select wrap text but as soon as you move away from the board and come back to it, it’s reset. Is there a way to fix this?

posts.author: michael_mbg (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml:
@amybronstein Hi Amy! Wanted to check on the latest timing for this.

Also, I saw you said there will be wrap text in cells. Wanted to confirm this will also be available in headers of columns.

Thanks!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Community! Thank you for your continued patience and feedback. I’m very excited to announce that the ability to wrap text within Long Text and Text Columns has been released! You are now able to display the text as “wrapped,” which will automatically adjust the height of your item to fit your text within the column. The wrap text preference will be saved automatically within a board view.

You can read more about the feature settings here. This feature will also be available in the Table Widget, and supported when exporting dashboards as a PDF file.

We understand there is still a need to wrap text within column headers, and we are exploring this further for the future. We’d love to hear more from you all! Please help share more use cases capturing how wrap text in column headers would be beneficial for your use case.

posts.author: michael_mbg (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @shannon-cxmonday The main request was for headers to have wrap text. Since this has not been released or resolved, can we please keep this open?

The column bodies wrapping for text cells is nice (although according to some comments, you have to select it every time you go back to the board) - but what is critical to this request is the headers.

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml:
Use case- My column headings have a lot of text and I need them to wrap

Also I need address columns and formula columns to wrap.

To be honest, I think I talk for many people here when I say that this “release” covered a small percent of the use cases/ requests on this thread. Not to take away from the appreciation of what has been done tho

posts.author: Lucas1 (Lucas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why is the status Released? The header of the feature request is litterally; “” Wrap text in column headings and/or cells" . Its not released!!!

Same thing with this feature request: Use Forms to edit/update existing items and columns - Feature requests - monday Community Forum People obviously wanted to be able to select an item in the form and then answer the questions, not create an item, look for it and then open the form and then answer the questions…

Incredibly frustrating to expect something is coming for years and then it turns out to be something else. I dont mind helping Monday improve their product but calling features released when theyre not is terrible practise.

posts.author: michael_mbg (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml:
@amber @Julietteb @BiancaT @amybronstein @shannon-cxmonday

Can we please reopen this request? The critical need was for Column Headings to have text wrap to save crucial space across boards. Please please please, reopen this! Otherwise we will have to start all over again and regain momentum with a new thread that (as this one has indicated) could take 4 years.

Thank you!

posts.author: Natasha.Duggal (Natasha)
posts.cookedHtml: Can we have this Wrap Text feature extend into the shareable link?

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to add my voice to the requests for column headings and changing the tag from “released”. Because

  1. Most importantly headings are THE way to navigate information. But having columns with for example single digits that are really wide just because the heading is longer is just a really bad UX. So wrapping column headings is much more important than wrapping cells.
  2. Wraping columns doesn’t even work for all kinds of columns (not even if you have one column that shows wrapped text)
  3. The provided solution is actually not that practical because item-rows are now sticky when opening subitems. So if you have a lot of text in the item you can barely see or read the subitem info.

posts.author: Jonathan-BookCheck (Jonathan Clayton)
posts.cookedHtml: Seconding the opinions that calling this “solved” is premature since there is still no option to wrap any/all column headings.

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to also second the “solution” is not complete. I still need header text wrapping.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: The Status has been changed to “coming soon”.

posts.author: Aliq21 (Christian Higgins)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature has been requested for 5 years and still hasn’t been added. That is sad

posts.author: MarkusPF (Markus Dick)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. Our main board has over 30 columns. Many of them just have simple numbers. However, the headers are so long that we have to navigate a lot, and it is hard to get a good project overview because we lose so much space to them. Please add this very simple feature!

posts.author: TPL (James Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d also like to add that the ability to wrap column headers would be very useful and it is disappointing that this relatively minor request has not been fulfilled. Perhaps monday.com should focus more on what it’s users are actually asking for rather than outputting more and more AI solutions that nobody really needs.
